title: podman inspect does not expose container dependency relationships
body:
## Problem

`podman inspect` does not expose relationships between containers, making it difficult to understand dependencies in multi-container setups.

For example:

```bash
podman run -d --name db postgres
podman run -d --name auth --label depends_on=db nginx
```

Running:

```bash
podman inspect auth
```

does not indicate that `auth` depends on `db`.

## Impact

* Harder debugging of service failures
* No visibility into dependency chains
* Risk when restarting or updating containers

## Proposed Solution

Expose container relationships in inspect output. As a minimal approach, dependencies can be derived from labels (e.g., `depends_on`) and surfaced as structured data.

## Notes

* This is a non-breaking enhancement
* Future work can extend this beyond labels (runtime-derived dependencies)

comments.body: see the comments on the PR but also please refer to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/LLM_POLICY.md
